core_case.question: Will the price of Solana be above $80 on April 19?
core_case.market_title: Will the price of Solana be above $80 on April 19?
core_case.contract_ambiguity_reason: minor implementation risk around exact Binance ET-noon minute/UI-vs-API mapping, though core rules are clear
core_case.freshness_driver: SOL distance from 80 and any crypto-wide risk-off move before the Apr 19 12:00 ET settlement minute
decision_blockers: Single-minute settlement makes ordinary path volatility more important than current spot
Independent verification of the large below-market edge is only medium strength
Final confidence is highly sensitive to a fresh Binance check closer to settlement

structured_handoff_primary.contract_ambiguity_reason: minor implementation risk around exact Binance ET-noon minute/UI-vs-API mapping, though core rules are clear
independently_verified_points: Polymarket rules specify Binance SOL/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute close with strict greater-than-80 condition
Direct Binance spot remained around 85.2 at synthesis time
Direct Binance recent one-minute data still showed SOL trading in the mid-85s
CoinGecko cross-check broadly matched Binance spot context
structured_handoff_primary.verification_gap_summary: The main remaining gap is direct empirical calibration of downside probability for the exact settlement minute over the next ~3 days.
structured_handoff_primary.best_countercase_summary: A normal crypto selloff or brief wick into the exact noon ET minute could still push SOL below 80 despite current mid-85s spot.
structured_handoff_primary.main_reason_for_disagreement: The main disagreement is volatility calibration for a single-minute settlement given a roughly 6% cushion above strike.
structured_handoff_primary.resolution_mechanics_summary: Yes resolves only if the Binance SOL/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute candle on April 19 closes strictly above 80.
structured_handoff_primary.freshness_driver: SOL distance from 80 and any crypto-wide risk-off move before the Apr 19 12:00 ET settlement minute
decision_blockers: Single-minute settlement makes ordinary path volatility more important than current spot
Independent verification of the large below-market edge is only medium strength
Final confidence is highly sensitive to a fresh Binance check closer to settlement

verification_enforcement.rationale: Decision-Maker should use the deterministic selected-input bundle as its verification boundary; additional agent-side tool use is disallowed in this mode.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: The market is directionally right because Binance already has SOL materially above 80, but exact-minute settlement and normal crypto volatility justify a modest haircut versus the 91.5% market price.
main_logical_chain: Check the governing venue and contract mechanics directly on Binance / Polymarket rules.
Observe that SOL is already comfortably above 80, so persistence is the main requirement.
Haircut the market slightly because exact-minute settlement and crypto volatility leave a nontrivial path to No.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance SOL/USDT read was about 85.27, already roughly $5.27 above strike.
Recent Binance 1-minute closes were clustered in the mid-85s rather than barely above 80.
strongest_disconfirmers: A ~6% drawdown over several days is plausible in crypto.
The contract settles on one exact 1-minute Binance close, not a daily average.
unresolved_ambiguities: How much realized volatility SOL will see between now and April 19.
Whether any weekend-style or event-driven move hits the exact settlement minute.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: I would move lower if SOL loses the 82-83 area or if a fresh Binance check near expiry shows the cushion over 80 has mostly vanished; I would move toward the market if SOL remains comfortably above 83-84 into April 18-19.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: The contract resolves at the Binance SOL/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute close on April 19, 2026, so exact-minute timing matters materially.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: High-confidence primary source-of-truth on rules and venue, with one useful secondary cross-check; independence is medium and source-of-truth ambiguity is low.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: Yes is more likely than No because recent Binance SOL/USDT trading has mostly been above $80, but the market's 91.5% price overstates confidence for a single-minute noon ET threshold contract.
main_logical_chain: Confirm the contract resolves using the Binance SOL/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute candle close on Apr 19.
Check recent Binance venue-specific SOL/USDT price distribution around the $80 threshold.
strongest_supports: Binance daily closes were above $80 on 13 of 15 completed days in the Apr 1-15 sample.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract resolves on one exact 12:00 ET one-minute close, so timing noise matters more than daily-level intuition.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact Binance UI candle labeling at the ET noon mapping was not independently verified beyond the contract rules.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: I would move up if SOL carries a larger cushion above $80 near settlement or if direct intraday noon-window evidence shows limited downside risk; I would move down if SOL loses the low-80s zone or broader crypto turns risk-off.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: High: the market resolves on a single one-minute close at 12:00 ET on Apr 19, 2026, so ordinary intraday volatility can dominate the outcome.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Good for a medium-difficulty run: direct market rules plus direct Binance venue data, but the contextual Binance check used daily rather than exact one-minute history.
